data.title: BGC Group beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• BGC Group press release (NASDAQ:BGC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.29 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $664.2M (+14.8% Y/Y) beats by $31.18M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $199.8 million, was 4.1 percent lower compared to last year due to a $36.6 million mark-to-market gain in the prior period, related to a firm investment. Excluding this gain from the prior period, Adjusted EBITDA would have increased by 16.3 percent.
  • Total brokerage revenues grew by 15.7 percent in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Q2 revenue consensus of $652.79M

data.title: B&G Foods Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 misses by $0.11, revenue of $425.4M misses by $33.93M
data.description:
• B&G Foods press release (NYSE:BGS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 misses by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $425.4M (-10.5% Y/Y) misses by $33.93M.
  • The decrease was primarily attributable to a decrease in volume, a decrease in net pricing and the impact of product mix, and the negative impact of foreign currency.

  • Guidance for Full Year Fiscal 2025

    • Net sales revised to a range of $1.86 billion to $1.91 billion.
    • Adjusted EBITDA revised to a range of $280.0 million to $290.0 million.
    • Adjusted diluted earnings per share revised to a range of $0.55 to $0.65.

data.title: Avadel Pharmaceuticals GAAP EPS of -$0.05
data.description:
• Avadel Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:AVDL): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.05.
  • Avadel recognized $52.5M in net product revenue from sales of LUMRYZ, a 93% increase compared to $27.2M for the same period in 2024.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were $66.5M at March 31, 2025 compared to $73.8M at December 31, 2024.
  • The company raised 2025 full-year guidance to $255M - $265M in net product revenue, cash operating expenses of $180M - $200M, and cash flow of $30M - $40M.

data.title: Burford Capital GAAP EPS of $0.14 misses by $0.16, revenue of $118.86M misses by $38.14M
data.description:
• Burford Capital press release (NYSE:BUR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.14 misses by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $118.86M (+168.3% Y/Y) misses by $38.14M.

data.title: International Money Express misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; lowers FY25 outlook and withdraws quarterly guidance
data.description:
• International Money Express press release (NASDAQ:IMXI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $144.3M (-4.1% Y/Y) misses by $2.93M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $21.6 million
  • The company's user base generated 12.8 million money transfer transactions, down 5.2% from last year. The total principal amount transferred for the period was $5.6 billion, an increase of 3.7%.

  • GuidanceBased on our first quarter 2025 financial results and the underlying market dynamics we have observed to date, the Company is revising its previously issued full-year guidance below. Current levels of uncertainty and volatility affecting market conditions and consumer behavior, have increased the difficulty of reliably forecasting short-term results.   Moreover, as previously announced, the Company is in the process of executing on a long-term strategy of investing in its digital business offerings to increase their contribution to the Company’s revenue and to increase its profitability.   Accordingly, the Company is discontinuing issuing quarterly guidance.

    Full-year 2025:•Revenue of $634.9 million to $654.2 million vs. $663.38M consensus (prior $657.5 million to $677.5 million).•Diluted EPS of $1.53 to $1.65.•Adjusted Diluted EPS of $1.86 to $2.02 vs. $2.17 consensus (prior $2.09 to $2.26).•Adjusted EBITDA of $103.6 million to $106.8 million.

data.title: Flex Non-GAAP EPS of $0.73 beats by $0.04, revenue of $6.4B beats by $170M
data.description:
• Flex press release (NASDAQ:FLEX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.73 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $6.4B (+3.2% Y/Y) beats by $170M.

  • First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Guidance

    • Revenue: $6.0 billion to $6.5 billion
    • GAAP Operating Income: $278 million to $318 million
    • Adjusted Operating Income: $330 million to $370 million
    • GAAP EPS: $0.46 to $0.54
    • Adjusted EPS: $0.58 to $0.66 which excludes $0.07 for net stock-based compensation expense and $0.05 for net intangible amortization.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance

    • Revenue: $25.0 billion to $26.8 billion
    • GAAP EPS: $2.35 to $2.55
    • Adjusted EPS: $2.81 to $3.01 which excludes $0.31 for net stock-based compensation expense and $0.15 for net intangible amortization.

data.title: Icahn Enterprises GAAP EPS of -$0.79, revenue of $2B misses by $630M
data.description:
• Icahn Enterprises press release (NASDAQ:IEP): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.79.
  • Revenue of $2B (-11.1% Y/Y) misses by $630M.
  • As of March 31, 2025, indicative net asset value decreased $336 million compared to December 31, 2024. The decreases was primarily due to losses in the Investment segment of $224 million, driven primarily by losses in the healthcare sector.

data.title: Teva rises as forex impact drives Q1 revenue miss
data.description:
Shares of Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) traded higher in the premarket on Wednesday after the generic drugmaker cited unfavorable forex movements as its revenue for the first quarter of 2025 fell short of Street forecasts.

The Israeli company posted $3.9B in revenue for the quarter with ~2% YoY growth, missing $3.99B in the consensus amid a $101M negative impact related to unfavorable exchange rate movements.

The company attributed the topline expansion to worldwide sales growth in its generic drugs and migraine product Ajovy, as well as U.S. growth for movement disorder therapy Austedo and schizophrenia drug Uzedy.

Teva’s (NYSE:TEVA) generic drugs added $2.3B to the topline with ~1% YoY growth globally, while Ajovy sales reached $139M with ~26% YoY growth in local currency terms. Meanwhile, Austedo and Uzedy sales from the U.S. grew ~40% YoY and ~156% YoY to $396M and $39M, respectively.

“Our key innovative growth drivers continue to show strong momentum, collectively generating revenues of $589 million while each growing more than 25% year over year,” CEO Richard Francis remarked. “We also achieved solid generics performance across all regions, with biosimilars rounding out the portfolio,” he added.

Meanwhile, TEVA’s adj. gross margin for the quarter improved to 52.8% from 51.4% in the prior-year period as the company’s non-GAAP diluted earnings grew ~8% YoY to $0.52 per share, exceeding $0.46 in the consensus.

The Tel Aviv-based drugmaker revised its full-year revenue and adjusted earnings outlook to $16.8B–$17.2B and $2.45–$2.65 per share, respectively, compared to $17.24B and $2.56 in the consensus and compared to $16.8B–$17.4B and $2.35–$2.65 in the prior forecast.

data.title: Pagaya stock surges after strong Q1 earnings point to full-year profitability
data.description:
Pagaya Technologies (NASDAQ:PGY) stock jumped 12% in Wednesday premarket trading after the AI-powered financial infrastructure company projected that it will be profitable this year after Q1 earnings and revenue both easily beat Wall Street estimates. The company raised its guidance for 2025 revenue, net income and adjusted EBITDA, even as it lowered expectations for network volume.

The company now expects 2025 net income of $10M-$45M compared with its prior guidance of -$10M to $40M and Visible Alpha consensus of $5.6M.  It also raised its guidance for full-year revenue and other income to $1.175B-$1.3B from prior range of $1.15B-$1.275B and vs. average analyst estimate of $1.20B. It projects adjusted EBITDA of $290M-$330M, vs. its prior guidance of $265M-$315M and Visible Alpha estimate of $283M. Network volume is now anticipated to be $9.5B-$11B, down from its prior outlook of $10.25B-$11.75B and compared with Visible Alpha estimate of $10.94B.

For Q2, Pagaya (NASDAQ:PGY) expects network volume of $2.3B-$2.5B vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $2.65B; total revenue and other income of $290M-$310M vs. $286M consensus; adjusted EBITDA of $75M-$90M vs. VA estimate of $67M; and GAAP net income between break-even and $10M vs. VA consensus of -$0.1M.

Q1 revenue and other income of $290.0M, beating the $285.1M consensus, increased from $245.3M in Q1 2024. 

Adjusted EPS of $0.69, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.46, rose from $0.20 a year ago.

"Pagaya has entered a new era in 2025 — an era of profitability. Our focus is clear: growing profit and creating long-term value for our shareholders while bridging Wall Street and Main Street," said CEO and co-founder Gal Krubiner.

Q1 network volume of $2.4B, missing the Visible Alpha estimate of $2.6B, was roughly flat with a year ago. Fee revenue less production cost, as a percentage, climbed to 4.8% vs. 3.8%.

Adjusted EBITDA of $79.6M, beating/missing the VA estimate of $67.4M, vs. $39.8M a year ago.

During Q1, Pagaya (PGY) raised $1.4B across three transactions and expanded its funding network by three new investors, for a total of 135 funding partners, with an additional two executed in Q2-to-date.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.
